title: Miles to Kilometers Converter 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Free miles to kilometers converter. 1 mi = 1.60934 km. Instant conversion both ways. Data updated 2026.
intro: Miles and kilometers are the two most common units of distance used worldwide. The **mile** (mi) is the standard unit in the United States, United Kingdom, and a handful of other countries, while the **kilometer** (km) dominates everywhere else. The exact conversion factor, defined by the international yard and pound agreement of 1959 and maintained by NIST, is **1 mile = 1.609344 kilometers** (exactly). Use this converter whenever you're reading a foreign road sign, planning a run in a country that uses different units, comparing vehicle fuel efficiency, or following an international recipe for a walking/cycling route. Unlike approximate rules-of-thumb, this tool applies the full 7-significant-figure factor so your result is accurate for engineering, sports timing, and navigation alike.
keyTakeaway: **1 mile = 1.609344 km exactly (NIST); conversely, 1 km = 0.621371 mi.** A 5-mile run is 8.047 km; a 100-km race is 62.137 miles.
seoKeywords: how many kilometers in a mile
useCases: Converting a US road-trip distance (e.g., 250 miles from NYC to Washington D.C.) into kilometers for a European friend's navigation app that only accepts metric input.
Translating a 10K race distance (10 km) into miles (6.214 mi) to set the correct treadmill distance at a US gym.
Calculating fuel efficiency comparisons: converting a car's EPA rating of 32 miles per gallon trip distance to kilometers to compare with a European vehicle's km/L spec.
Checking whether a hiking trail listed as 14.5 km in a Canadian park map exceeds the 8-mile daily limit recommended by your tour operator.
Converting speed-limit signs when driving a rental car in the UK (e.g., 70 mph motorway limit → 112.65 km/h) to verify compliance with a GPS set to metric.
Preparing for a marathon: confirming that 26.219 miles equals exactly 42.195 km, the IAAF-certified distance, before registering for an international race.
explanation:
## How It Is Calculated

The conversion is based on the **exact, legally-defined ratio** established by the International Yard and Pound Agreement (1959), adopted by NIST:

```
Miles → Kilometers:
  km = mi × 1.609344

Kilometers → Miles:
  mi = km ÷ 1.609344
     = km × 0.6213711922...
```

The factor 1.609344 is **exact** (not a rounded approximation). It derives from:  
1 mile = 1,760 yards; 1 yard = 0.9144 m (exact) → 1,760 × 0.9144 = 1,609.344 m = 1.609344 km.

---

## Reference Table

| Miles (mi) | Kilometers (km) | Common context |
|-----------|----------------|----------------|
| 0.25 | 0.402 | Quarter-mile drag strip |
| 0.5 | 0.805 | Half-mile track |
| 1 | 1.609 | 1-mile run benchmark |
| 3.107 | 5.000 | 5K race in miles |
| 5 | 8.047 | Common morning run |
| 6.214 | 10.000 | 10K race in miles |
| 13.109 | 21.098 | Half marathon |
| 26.219 | 42.195 | Full marathon (IAAF) |
| 50 | 80.467 | Short road trip |
| 100 | 160.934 | Century bike ride |
| 238,855 | 384,400 | Earth–Moon average distance |

---

## Typical Cases

### Case 1 — Road trip planning (mi → km)
A driver in Texas plans a 350-mile trip to New Mexico and wants to enter the distance into a metric GPS:

> 350 mi × 1.609344 = **563.27 km**

### Case 2 — Trail running (km → mi)
A trail race in British Columbia is advertised as 32 km. An American runner wants to know the mileage:

> 32 km ÷ 1.609344 = **19.884 mi** (≈ 19.9 miles)

### Case 3 — Speed conversion (mph → km/h)
Because speed is distance per unit time, the same factor applies:  
A US interstate limit of 75 mph converted to km/h:

> 75 mph × 1.609344 = **120.70 km/h**

---

## Common Errors

1. **Using 1.6 instead of 1.609344** — The rounded factor 1.6 introduces a 0.58% error. Over 100 miles this is a 580-meter discrepancy — enough to matter in navigation and race timing.

2. **Confusing nautical miles with statute miles** — 1 nautical mile = 1.852 km, not 1.609344 km. Aviation and maritime distances use nautical miles; applying the road-mile factor gives results ~15% off.

3. **Inverting the direction** — Multiplying km by 1.609344 (instead of dividing) when converting km → mi overstates the mileage by ~159%. Always divide (or multiply by 0.621371) when going km → mi.

4. **Rounding too early in multi-step calculations** — If you first round 1.609 and then multiply by several values (e.g., for a fuel calculation), errors compound. Keep full precision until the final step.

5. **Mixing up miles per hour with kilometers per hour in vehicle specs** — A car listed at "100" in Europe means 100 km/h (62.1 mph), not 100 mph. This error has safety implications, especially for rental-car drivers.

---

## Related Calculators

Since there are no internal related calculators configured for this tool, explore other unit converters on Hacé Cuentas for length, speed, and weight conversions to complete your measurement toolkit.

faq.q: What is the exact conversion factor from miles to kilometers?
faq.a: The exact factor is **1 mile = 1.609344 kilometers**, with no rounding. This was fixed by the International Yard and Pound Agreement of 1959 and is the standard used by NIST. The decimal never repeats or terminates beyond the six digits shown because it is a defined value, not a measured one.

faq.q: How do I convert kilometers back to miles?
faq.a: Divide by 1.609344, or equivalently multiply by 0.6213711922. For example, 50 km ÷ 1.609344 = **31.069 miles**. The recurring decimal 0.62137… is the inverse of the exact mile definition and can be rounded to 0.6214 for most everyday purposes.

faq.q: Is a nautical mile the same as a regular mile?
faq.a: No. A **statute mile** (the land mile) = 1.609344 km, while a **nautical mile** = exactly **1.852 km** (defined by the International Hydrographic Organization). Nautical miles are used in aviation and maritime navigation and are about 15% longer than statute miles. Never apply the 1.609344 factor to nautical-mile distances.

faq.q: Why do the US and UK still use miles instead of kilometers?
faq.a: The US and UK retained the mile through historical inertia and political resistance to full metrication. The US Metric Conversion Act of 1975 made metrication voluntary, so road signage stayed in miles. The UK partially metricated but kept miles for road distances by law. Both countries use km in science, medicine, and some industries.

faq.q: How many miles is a 5K, 10K, and marathon in kilometers?
faq.a: Using the exact IAAF-certified distances: a **5K = 3.107 mi**, a **10K = 6.214 mi**, a half marathon (21.0975 km) = **13.109 mi**, and a full marathon = **26.219 mi** (42.195 km exactly). These are the distances used for official race timing and world records.

faq.q: Does this converter work for speed (mph to km/h)?
faq.a: Yes — since speed is distance per hour, the same factor applies directly: **km/h = mph × 1.609344**. For example, the US federal highway speed limit of 70 mph = 112.65 km/h. The reverse: 130 km/h (common European motorway limit) = 80.78 mph.

faq.q: How accurate is the 'multiply by 1.6' mental shortcut?
faq.a: The shortcut gives a result about **0.58% lower** than the true value. Over short distances (1–5 miles) the error is negligible (under 50 m). Over 100 miles the shortcut yields 160 km vs. the correct 160.934 km — nearly 1 km off. For navigation, sports, or engineering, always use the full 1.609344 factor.

faq.q: What is the distance in miles from the Earth to the Moon?
faq.a: The Moon's average distance is **238,855 miles**, which equals approximately **384,400 km** (238,855 × 1.609344). This is one of the largest everyday applications of the miles-to-km conversion and is cited by NASA using both unit systems in public communications.

sources.name: NIST – Appendix B: Conversion Factors (mile, international)
